items.title: Norms and distance foundations
items.description: L1, L2 and the definition d(u,v) = ||u − v|| live in Norms & Distances; this topic applies those foundations to algorithm and library choices.

items.title: k-Nearest Neighbors
items.description: k-NN classification and regression rank training points entirely by the chosen metric; metric and k should be tuned together on validation data.

items.title: SVM kernels and implicit geometry
items.description: The RBF kernel exp(−γ||u−v||²) embeds squared Euclidean distance into similarity; kernel choice is a related but distinct geometry decision from explicit k-NN metrics.

items.title: k-means clustering
items.description: Standard k-means minimizes within-cluster sum of squared Euclidean distances; alternative metrics require different clustering algorithms.

items.title: Feature scaling before distances
items.description: Unscaled columns distort Minkowski and Euclidean rankings; preprocess features so each coordinate contributes meaningfully to the metric.
